# MariaDB TPC-C — sweep parameters that need a server restart

## Benchmark

BenchBase's TPC-C workload. Five transaction types (`NewOrder`, `Payment`,
`OrderStatus`, `Delivery`, `StockLevel`) in a `45/43/4/4/4` mix against a
wholesale-supplier schema. The score is steady-state TPS at N concurrent
terminals.

| | |
|---|---|
| Scale factor | 200 warehouses (~14 GB on disk) |
| Terminals | 1, 10, 30 |
| Per trial | 90 s warm-up + 180 s measurement |
| Trials per cell | 3, with seed snapshot + MariaDB restart between trials |
| Isolation | `READ_COMMITTED` |
| Rate cap | 10 000 req/s |
| Host | 32-core, 60 GB RAM, single NVMe, Fedora 43 |
| MariaDB | 10.11 in a Docker container |
| BenchBase | `cmu-db/benchbase`, `mariadb` build profile |

A throwaway `_prewarm` variant runs once before the first measured variant so
the OS page cache, InnoDB internal state and JVM JIT are in a representative
state when the first measurement starts.

## Task coverage

| Task point | Done as |
|---|---|
| Local MariaDB | `mariadb:10.11` container, full root over `/etc/my.cnf`, server restart between every variant |
| TPC-C | BenchBase `tpcc` workload, default weights `45,43,4,4,4` |
| BenchBase | `cmu-db/benchbase`, `mariadb` profile |
| Tuning | 17 variants across 4 InnoDB parameters; 2 of them need a server restart |
| Parameter effect | Tables and chart below |
| Sensownie | 3 trials per cell, seed restore between trials, throwaway warm-up variant first |

## Variants

| Variant | `my.cnf` delta from MariaDB 10.11 defaults |
|---|---|
| `baseline/defaults` | none |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_128m` | `innodb_buffer_pool_size = 128M` (default) |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_1g`   | `innodb_buffer_pool_size = 1G` |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_4g`   | `innodb_buffer_pool_size = 4G` |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_8g`   | `innodb_buffer_pool_size = 8G` |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_16g`  | `innodb_buffer_pool_size = 16G` |
| `log_file_size_sweep/log_96m`  | `innodb_log_file_size = 96M` (default), `bp = 4G` |
| `log_file_size_sweep/log_512m` | `innodb_log_file_size = 512M`, `bp = 4G` |
| `log_file_size_sweep/log_2g`   | `innodb_log_file_size = 2G`, `bp = 4G` |
| `flush_log_at_trx_commit_sweep/trx_1_acid`    | `innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit = 1` (ACID, default), `bp = 4G` |
| `flush_log_at_trx_commit_sweep/trx_2_relaxed` | `innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit = 2` (write per commit, fsync 1 Hz), `bp = 4G` |
| `flush_log_at_trx_commit_sweep/trx_0_relaxed` | `innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit = 0` (write + fsync 1 Hz), `bp = 4G` |
| `flush_method_sweep/flush_o_direct` | `innodb_flush_method = O_DIRECT` (default), `bp = 4G` |
| `flush_method_sweep/flush_fsync`    | `innodb_flush_method = fsync`, `bp = 4G` |
| `tuned/best_combo`        | `bp=8G`, `log=2G`, `trx=2`, `O_DIRECT`, `io_capacity=2000/4000`, `log_buffer_size=64M` |
| `tuned/tuned_minus_log`   | `best_combo` with `log_file_size` back to `96M` |
| `tuned/tuned_minus_iotune`| `best_combo` without the `io_capacity` and `log_buffer_size` bumps |

## TPS (mean ± 1 std across 3 trials, sorted by t=10)

| Variant | t=1 | t=10 | t=30 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **`tuned/best_combo`** | **2011.3 ±  11.8** | **6600.7 ±  87.5** | **4197.3 ± 138.4** |
| `tuned/tuned_minus_iotune` |  2011.2 ±   9.4 |  6487.6 ±  16.4 |  4166.5 ±  19.4 |
| `log_file_size_sweep/log_2g` |   315.8 ±   1.4 |  1300.6 ± 252.3 |  2116.4 ±  43.3 |
| `log_file_size_sweep/log_512m` |   317.2 ±   1.0 |  1109.1 ± 140.5 |  1298.2 ±   9.7 |
| `flush_log_at_trx_commit_sweep/trx_0_relaxed` |  1554.8 ±  17.3 |  1089.0 ±  40.2 |   627.9 ±   8.7 |
| `tuned/tuned_minus_log` |  1511.2 ±   2.6 |  1047.8 ±   9.3 |   639.3 ±   3.8 |
| `flush_log_at_trx_commit_sweep/trx_2_relaxed` |  1521.1 ±  21.9 |  1047.7 ±  17.8 |   631.9 ±  11.9 |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_4g` |   311.6 ±   1.4 |   720.4 ±  87.4 |   571.9 ±   4.6 |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_8g` |   305.1 ±   8.9 |   712.5 ±  66.4 |   577.9 ±   3.2 |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_16g` |   310.9 ±   2.3 |   693.8 ±  88.0 |   579.1 ±   4.4 |
| `flush_log_at_trx_commit_sweep/trx_1_acid` |   305.1 ±   6.4 |   675.6 ±  88.6 |   578.9 ±   3.0 |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_1g` |   304.9 ±   1.3 |   671.2 ±  31.5 |   461.9 ±   0.8 |
| `log_file_size_sweep/log_96m` |   311.1 ±   0.3 |   665.8 ±  30.4 |   577.6 ±   1.7 |
| `flush_method_sweep/flush_o_direct` |   309.4 ±   0.1 |   664.3 ±  32.4 |   592.5 ±  20.1 |
| `flush_method_sweep/flush_fsync` |   266.3 ±  75.8 |   634.5 ±   4.7 |   616.6 ±   3.3 |
| `baseline/defaults` |   225.7 ±  56.1 |   241.0 ±  11.6 |   177.8 ±   4.4 |
| `buffer_pool_sweep/bp_128m` |   270.7 ±   1.6 |   236.6 ±  10.1 |   174.9 ±   0.8 |

## Throughput per variant at each terminal count

Rows are variants in sweep order, columns are the three terminal counts, cell colour and number show mean TPS across the three trials, subscript shows ±1 std. White hairlines separate sweeps.

![TPS by variant and terminal count](data:image/png;base64,)

## Tuned decomposition (t=10, trial 0)

| Config | TPS | log writes / s | log MB / s |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| `trx_2` alone (bp=4G, log=96M) | 1029 | 2494 | 15.5 |
| `log_2g` alone (bp=4G, trx=1) | 1378 | 588 | 10.9 |
| `tuned_minus_log` (bp=8G, log=96M, trx=2) | 1054 | 2497 | 15.4 |
| **`best_combo` (bp=8G, log=2G, trx=2)** | 6696 | 8538 | 52.8 |
| `tuned_minus_iotune` (bp=8G, log=2G, trx=2) | 6495 | 8259 | 51.2 |

`tuned_minus_log` matches `trx_2` alone on both TPS and log writes/s; the
extra buffer pool and `io_capacity` bumps in `best_combo` add nothing while
the 96 M log keeps the checkpointer saturated. Restoring
`innodb_log_file_size = 2G` lets the same configuration sustain 8 538 log
writes/s — 3.4× more — and pushes TPS from 1 054 to 6 696.

Minimum tuned recipe:

```
innodb_buffer_pool_size        = 8G
innodb_log_file_size           = 2G
innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit = 2
```

## Caveats

Scale 200 (~14 GB) versus the upstream 1 000 (~70 GB). Terminal grid is
sparse (1, 10, 30). `baseline/defaults` at t=1 and
`flush_method_sweep/flush_fsync` at t=1 each have one outlier trial pulling
the std to ~25 %; the other 142 cells are within 5–15 % RSD.
